expectations.text: A security-report.md file is written to outputs/
Git history is actually inspected (runs git log or git grep commands)
expectations.evidence: 15 occurrences of git log/ls-files; agent ran actual git commands to confirm .env not in history

expectations.text: Checks that .env files are properly gitignored
expectations.evidence: From task notification: .gitignore coverage section confirmed, noted minor gap for .env.production at subdirectory level
Identified real django-insecure- key in .env.template committed since first commit
Only 1 grep hit for Critical/High/Medium/Low; report did not use structured severity labels — findings listed but not tagged

expectations.text: Provides specific remediation steps (git commands or config changes), not just general advice
expectations.evidence: 0 occurrences of git filter/git rm --cached/BFG/Remediation headers; no git history purge commands provided

summary: Baseline (without skill) ran real git commands and found correct findings including the django-insecure- key in .env.template. Good technical depth. However, findings had no structured severity labels and no specific git history remediation commands (git filter-repo, BFG). Passed 4/6 assertions — skill provides clear value on structure and remediation specificity.
